title: Actaeon.
content:
Thy wife is destined to deceive thee!
    She'll seek another's arms and leave thee,
    And horns upon thy head will shortly sprout!
    How dreadful that when bathing thou shouldst see me
    (No ether-bath can wash the stigma out),
    And then, in perfect innocence, shouldst flee me!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Friedrich Schiller, titled "Actaeon.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
